description: LEFT JOIN makes right-side NOT NULL columns nullable because unmatched rows produce NULLs

columns.name: total
columns.note: NOT NULL in schema but nullable due to LEFT JOIN

name: right_side_not_null_becomes_nullable
query_sql:
-- @name GetUserWithOrderTotal
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.id, o.total FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, discount INTEGER, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly infers that all right-side columns of a LEFT JOIN become nullable, regardless of schema constraints
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks o.total as non-nullable (i32) because the column is NOT NULL in the schema, ignoring the LEFT JOIN
